items.title: Z.ai shipped GLM 5.2
items.oneliner: Z.ai says GLM 5.2 brings 1M context and coding performance near closed leaders, making open deployment harder to dismiss.

items.title: Switzerland launched Apertus sovereign models
items.oneliner: Apertus ships open 8B and 70B sovereign models with data transparency and EU AI Act alignment baked in.

items.title: Sakana productized multi-agent orchestration
items.oneliner: Sakana's Fugu turns model routing into a product, signaling that orchestration is becoming a first-class layer, not a hack.

items.title: OpenAI o3 found rare disease diagnoses
items.oneliner: OpenAI says o3 helped confirm 18 diagnoses across 376 unsolved pediatric cases, a concrete clinical win beyond chatbot demos.

items.title: Codex may flood local SSDs
items.oneliner: An OpenAI Codex logging bug may write terabytes to local SSDs, reminding teams that agent tooling can fail expensively.

items.title: Danish police raided a privacy activist
items.oneliner: Armed police raided a Danish privacy activist over leaked official phone numbers, showing encryption politics now carries real force.

items.title: Linux Secure Boot faces key rollover
items.oneliner: Linux distributions must handle Microsoft's Secure Boot key expiry, or future installs could fail on perfectly healthy hardware.

items.title: Datadog quantified AI engineering economics
items.oneliner: Datadog's 2026 AI engineering report puts token costs and provider shifts in telemetry, giving teams numbers instead of vibes.

items.title: OpenAI added record-and-replay for Codex
items.oneliner: OpenAI's new Codex record-and-replay turns one demonstrated workflow into a reusable skill, pushing agents toward actual operations.

items.title: Deno moved into desktop apps
items.oneliner: Deno Desktop canary packages web apps into native binaries with in-process communication, taking aim at Electron's size tax.
